references.cue: Linear’s March 12, 2026 UI refresh calls out calmer headers, consistent controls, and dimmer navigation sidebars so the main work surface carries the attention.
references.adapted: The attachment and thread layer keeps restrained chrome, thin borders, and a dense reading plane so the artifact or chronology stays dominant.
references.rejected: I did not copy Linear’s issue-detail density or document-editor framing because the workspace still needs overt trust and continuity cues.

references.cue: Vercel’s redesigned dashboard navigation keeps persistent section chrome while letting the main content area take focus.
references.adapted: The viewer opens as a bounded same-shell side stage rather than replacing the task plane, and the return path stays visible.
references.rejected: I did not introduce another workspace-wide navigation layer because route-family ownership already lives in the existing shell.

references.cue: The dashboard overview emphasizes persistent search, saved view preference, and project detail that opens without discarding the surrounding context.
references.adapted: The patient-response thread and artifact viewer preserve the current shell and quiet-return target instead of opening a second inbox or file center.
references.rejected: I did not borrow Vercel’s project-card or grid/list toggles because the task shell already has its own queue grammar.

references.cue: Carbon’s data-table guidance stresses distinct click targets, hover-assisted scanning, zebra or alternating rhythm, and skeleton states over spinners when table data reloads.
references.adapted: Thread rows use discrete focus targets, clear chips, and steady vertical rhythm rather than chat bubbles or overloaded cards.
references.rejected: I did not convert the thread into a literal table because chronology needs stacked narrative rows, not columnar grid chrome.

references.cue: Carbon recommends skeleton and progressive loading for heavy content, especially when table or card data takes time to hydrate.
references.adapted: Audio and other heavier artifacts use chunked loading with summary and provenance left in place, so the task shell never looks frozen.
references.rejected: I did not use blank spinner takeovers or modal overlays because they would break the workspace continuity law.

references.cue: NHS guidance emphasizes visible focus, skip links, clear headings, informative titles, and avoiding hidden or ambiguous controls.
references.adapted: The thread and viewer surfaces keep explicit labels, clear focus targets, and visible return actions instead of icon-only or toast-only cues.
references.rejected: I did not rely on color or position alone to communicate repair, visibility, or recovery state.

references.cue: Question pages reinforce one dominant ask at a time and require a back path that preserves the last seen state.
references.adapted: The thread panel keeps one dominant next action and one clear return path while older chronology anchors remain recoverable rather than disappearing.
references.rejected: I did not turn the staff chronology into a multi-step wizard because the reviewer needs persistent context, not page-by-page capture flow.
